---
title: "Recency Based Email Targeting: Implementation"
---

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)

# Output folders. Plots go under plots/ 
dir.create("plots", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("tables", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("output", showWarnings = FALSE)
```

```{r load-data}
data <- read_csv("data/hillstrom_data.csv")
```

# Section 1: Raw data description

```{r section1-quality-checks}
# Required descriptive checks per Section 1.
n_rows <- nrow(data)
n_cols <- ncol(data)
n_dupes <- sum(duplicated(data))

segment_counts <- count(data, segment)
visit_counts <- count(data, visit) |> mutate(share = n / sum(n))
conversion_counts <- count(data, conversion) |> mutate(share = n / sum(n))

missing_counts <- data |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "missing")

# Data-quality flags (the plan asks us to record warnings if any fail).
quality_warnings <- character()

if (!setequal(unique(data$segment),
              c("No E-Mail", "Mens E-Mail", "Womens E-Mail"))) {
  quality_warnings <- c(quality_warnings,
                        "segment levels deviate from the expected three arms")
}
if (!all(data$visit %in% c(0, 1))) {
  quality_warnings <- c(quality_warnings, "visit not strictly 0/1")
}
if (!all(data$conversion %in% c(0, 1))) {
  quality_warnings <- c(quality_warnings, "conversion not strictly 0/1")
}
if (any(data$spend < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  quality_warnings <- c(quality_warnings, "spend has negative values")
}
if (any(data$recency < 1 | data$recency > 12, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  quality_warnings <- c(quality_warnings,
                        "recency falls outside the expected 1-12 range")
}
if (any(data$history < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  quality_warnings <- c(quality_warnings, "history has negative values")
}
for (v in c("mens", "womens", "newbie")) {
  if (!all(data[[v]] %in% c(0, 1))) {
    quality_warnings <- c(quality_warnings, sprintf("%s not strictly 0/1", v))
  }
}

list(n_rows = n_rows, n_cols = n_cols, n_dupes = n_dupes,
     segment_counts = segment_counts, visit_counts = visit_counts,
     conversion_counts = conversion_counts,
     quality_warnings = quality_warnings)
```

```{r section1-table1}
# Table 1: per-variable summary stats. Column set is fixed by the plan.
summary_vars <- c("visit", "conversion", "spend",
                  "recency", "history", "mens", "womens", "newbie")

table1 <- map_dfr(summary_vars, function(v) {
  x <- data[[v]]
  tibble(
    variable = v,
    n        = length(x),
    missing  = sum(is.na(x)),
    mean     = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd       = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    min      = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median   = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    max      = max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
})

write_csv(table1, "tables/table1_raw_summary.csv")
```

# Section 2: Sample construction plan

```{r section2-recency-bins}
# Recency bins per the plan: 3-month lifecycle windows from Warm to Very dormant.
# recency_out_of_range flags any value outside 1-12 so we can report them
# without forcing them into a bin.
data <- data |>
  mutate(
    recency_out_of_range = recency < 1 | recency > 12,
    recency_bin = case_when(
      recency >= 1  & recency <= 3  ~ "Warm",
      recency >= 4  & recency <= 6  ~ "Cooling",
      recency >= 7  & recency <= 9  ~ "Dormant",
      recency >= 10 & recency <= 12 ~ "Very dormant",
      TRUE                           ~ NA_character_
    ),
    recency_bin = factor(recency_bin,
                         levels = c("Warm", "Cooling", "Dormant", "Very dormant"),
                         ordered = TRUE)
  )

n_out <- sum(data$recency_out_of_range, na.rm = TRUE)
n_unbinned <- sum(is.na(data$recency_bin))
list(n_out_of_range = n_out, n_excluded_from_bin_analyses = n_unbinned)
```

```{r section2-treatment-indicators}
# Plan's treatment indicators. No E-Mail remains the implicit reference.
data <- data |>
  mutate(
    mens_email   = as.integer(segment == "Mens E-Mail"),
    womens_email = as.integer(segment == "Womens E-Mail")
  )
```

```{r section2-winsorized-spend}
# Winsorize at the 99th percentile of the full analysis sample (NOT by arm
# or by recency bin, per the plan). Used in robustness only — the main
# analysis uses raw spend.
spend_p99 <- quantile(data$spend, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)
data <- data |> mutate(spend_w99 = pmin(spend, spend_p99))
spend_p99
```

```{r section2-table2}
# Table 2: per-bin × arm cell counts. The plan emphasizes this matters
# because the credibility of subgroup effects depends on cell size.
table2 <- data |>
  filter(!is.na(recency_bin)) |>
  count(recency_bin, segment, name = "n") |>
  group_by(recency_bin) |>
  mutate(share_of_bin = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup()

write_csv(table2, "tables/table2_recency_arm_counts.csv")
```

```{r section2-figure-cell-counts}
#| label: fig-cell-counts
#| fig-cap: "Per-(recency bin × arm) cell counts. Red dashed line at n = 500 marks the credibility threshold from the plan's robustness check (cells below it carry weak subgroup estimates). All cells comfortably exceed the threshold."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5

# Visualizing Table 2 (cell counts) as a grouped bar plot. This figure does
# double duty: it's the Section 2 cell-count display AND the Analysis 4
# Check 1 cell-size diagnostic — both questions are answered by the same
# data with a 500-customer reference line.
fig_cells <- table2 |>
  mutate(
    segment     = factor(segment,
                         levels = c("No E-Mail", "Mens E-Mail", "Womens E-Mail")),
    recency_bin = factor(recency_bin,
                         levels = c("Warm", "Cooling", "Dormant", "Very dormant"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = recency_bin, y = n, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 500, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(n)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "No E-Mail"     = "grey60",
    "Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
    "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.10)),
                     labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  labs(
    title    = "Cell sizes: customers per recency bin and arm",
    subtitle = "Red dashed line at n = 500 marks the credibility threshold for subgroup estimates",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Customers (n)",
    fill     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

ggsave("plots/figure_cell_counts.png",
       fig_cells, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

# Treatment-effect helper

```{r te-helper}
# Single helper used by every subsequent analysis. Implements the plan's
# difference-in-means formula with two-sample SE; works for binary or
# continuous outcomes.
te_row <- function(df, outcome, t_label, c_label, comparison_label) {
  yt <- df[[outcome]][df$segment == t_label]
  yc <- df[[outcome]][df$segment == c_label]
  if (length(yt) < 2 || length(yc) < 2) return(NULL)
  est <- mean(yt) - mean(yc)
  se  <- sqrt(var(yt) / length(yt) + var(yc) / length(yc))
  tibble(
    comparison      = comparison_label,
    outcome         = outcome,
    estimate        = est,
    se              = se,
    ci_low          = est - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high         = est + 1.96 * se,
    n_treatment     = length(yt),
    n_comparison    = length(yc),
    mean_treatment  = mean(yt),
    mean_comparison = mean(yc)
  )
}

# Standard list of three pairwise comparisons. Reused throughout.
comparisons <- list(
  list(t = "Mens E-Mail",   c = "No E-Mail",     label = "Mens E-Mail minus No E-Mail"),
  list(t = "Womens E-Mail", c = "No E-Mail",     label = "Womens E-Mail minus No E-Mail"),
  list(t = "Mens E-Mail",   c = "Womens E-Mail", label = "Mens E-Mail minus Womens E-Mail")
)
```

# Baseline analysis: Overall treatment effects

```{r baseline-table3}
# Table 3: overall treatment effects on spend (full sample, no recency split).
table3 <- map_dfr(comparisons, function(cmp) {
  te_row(data, "spend", cmp$t, cmp$c, cmp$label)
})

write_csv(table3, "tables/table3_overall_treatment_effects.csv")
```

```{r baseline-figure0}
#| label: fig-0
#| fig-cap: "Mean spend per customer by treatment arm with 95% CIs and dollar / percent lift annotations versus No E-Mail."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

# Figure 0: 3-bar chart of arm means with CI whiskers and lift annotations.
arm_means <- data |>
  group_by(segment) |>
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    mean_spend = mean(spend),
    se         = sd(spend) / sqrt(n),
    ci_low     = mean_spend - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high    = mean_spend + 1.96 * se,
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(segment = factor(segment,
                          levels = c("No E-Mail", "Mens E-Mail", "Womens E-Mail")))

control_mean <- arm_means |> filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |> pull(mean_spend)

arm_means <- arm_means |>
  mutate(
    abs_diff = mean_spend - control_mean,
    pct_lift = abs_diff / control_mean * 100,
    label    = if_else(segment == "No E-Mail", "Control",
                       sprintf("+$%.2f\n(%+.1f%%)", abs_diff, pct_lift))
  )

fig0 <- ggplot(arm_means, aes(x = segment, y = mean_spend, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = control_mean, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_high, label = label), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "No E-Mail"     = "grey60",
    "Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
    "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.20)),
                     labels = dollar_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Overall treatment effects on spend",
    subtitle = "Mean spend per customer by arm (95% CIs)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean spend per customer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

ggsave("plots/figure0_overall_spend_by_arm.png",
       fig0, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

# Analysis 1: Main recency spend lift figure

```{r analysis1-table4}
# Table 4: per-(recency bin, comparison) treatment effect on spend.
# Uses the same three comparisons as the baseline, repeated within each bin.
table4 <- data |>
  filter(!is.na(recency_bin)) |>
  group_by(recency_bin) |>
  group_modify(~ map_dfr(comparisons, function(cmp) {
    te_row(.x, "spend", cmp$t, cmp$c, cmp$label)
  })) |>
  ungroup()

write_csv(table4, "tables/table4_recency_spend_lift.csv")
```

```{r analysis1-figure1-faceted-bars}
#| label: fig-1-faceted-bars
#| fig-cap: "Mean spend per customer by arm, faceted by recency bin. Within each panel: 3 bars with 95% CIs, dashed reference line at the bin's No E-Mail mean, dollar/percent lift annotations above each email bar. Same visual idiom as Figure 0 (the baseline) repeated for each lifecycle window."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7

# Alternative presentation of Figure 1 that reuses the Figure 0 idiom
# (3 bars + CIs + dashed control reference + lift annotations) repeated per
# recency bin. Saved alongside the forest plot — does NOT replace it. Use
# this for audience presentations; keep the forest plot for the writeup
# appendix where forest plots are conventional.
arm_means_by_bin <- data |>
  filter(!is.na(recency_bin)) |>
  group_by(recency_bin, segment) |>
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    mean_spend = mean(spend),
    se         = sd(spend) / sqrt(n),
    ci_low     = mean_spend - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high    = mean_spend + 1.96 * se,
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    segment     = factor(segment,
                         levels = c("No E-Mail", "Mens E-Mail", "Womens E-Mail")),
    recency_bin = factor(recency_bin,
                         levels = c("Warm", "Cooling", "Dormant", "Very dormant"))
  )

# Per-bin control mean drives both the dashed reference line and the lift
# annotations — each panel uses ITS OWN bin's No E-Mail mean as the anchor,
# not the global mean.
control_means_by_bin <- arm_means_by_bin |>
  filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |>
  select(recency_bin, control_mean = mean_spend)

arm_means_by_bin <- arm_means_by_bin |>
  left_join(control_means_by_bin, by = "recency_bin") |>
  mutate(
    abs_diff = mean_spend - control_mean,
    pct_lift = abs_diff / control_mean * 100,
    label    = if_else(segment == "No E-Mail",
                       "Control",
                       sprintf("+$%.2f\n(%+.0f%%)", abs_diff, pct_lift))
  )

# Fixed y-scale (NOT free) so cross-panel bar heights are comparable.
# Warm customers genuinely spend more on average than Very dormant ones,
# and an honest visual should preserve that contrast — free_y would hide it.
fig1_faceted <- ggplot(arm_means_by_bin,
                       aes(x = segment, y = mean_spend, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(data = control_means_by_bin,
             aes(yintercept = control_mean),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_high, label = label),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3.2, lineheight = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ recency_bin, nrow = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "No E-Mail"     = "grey60",
    "Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
    "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25)),
                     labels = dollar_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Spend lift by recency bin and email arm",
    subtitle = "Mean spend per customer with 95% CIs; dashed line = each bin's No E-Mail baseline",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean spend per customer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text     = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 10)
  )

ggsave("plots/figure1_recency_spend_faceted_bars.png",
       fig1_faceted, width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

# Analysis 2: Recency-based causal funnel decomposition

```{r analysis2-table5}
# Table 5: per-(bin, outcome, comparison) treatment effect on the three
# funnel outcomes. Mens-vs-Womens direct comparison is omitted here per
# the plan (the funnel is keyed on email-vs-no-email, not Mens-vs-Womens).
funnel_outcomes <- c("visit", "conversion", "spend")
funnel_comparisons <- comparisons[1:2]

table5 <- data |>
  filter(!is.na(recency_bin)) |>
  group_by(recency_bin) |>
  group_modify(~ {
    df <- .x
    crossing(outcome = funnel_outcomes,
             cmp_idx = seq_along(funnel_comparisons)) |>
      pmap_dfr(function(outcome, cmp_idx) {
        cmp <- funnel_comparisons[[cmp_idx]]
        te_row(df, outcome, cmp$t, cmp$c, cmp$label)
      })
  }) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    estimate_display = if_else(outcome %in% c("visit", "conversion"),
                               estimate * 100, estimate),
    ci_low_display   = if_else(outcome %in% c("visit", "conversion"),
                               ci_low * 100, ci_low),
    ci_high_display  = if_else(outcome %in% c("visit", "conversion"),
                               ci_high * 100, ci_high),
    units = if_else(outcome %in% c("visit", "conversion"),
                    "percentage points",
                    "dollars per assigned customer")
  ) |>
  select(recency_bin, comparison, outcome,
         estimate, estimate_display,
         se, ci_low, ci_high, ci_low_display, ci_high_display,
         n_treatment, n_comparison, mean_treatment, mean_comparison, units)

write_csv(table5, "tables/table5_recency_funnel_lift.csv")
```

```{r analysis2-faceted-bars-helper}
# Shared helper: build the per-(bin, arm) means + CIs + per-bin reference
# means used by all three faceted-bar funnel figures below. Same SE formula
# works for spend (continuous) and visit/conversion (binary 0/1) because
# var(0/1) = p(1-p) up to a finite-sample correction — sd()/sqrt(n) gives
# the right SE in both cases at this sample size.
build_funnel_arm_means <- function(outcome) {
  arm_means <- data |>
    filter(!is.na(recency_bin)) |>
    group_by(recency_bin, segment) |>
    summarise(
      n       = n(),
      mean_y  = mean(.data[[outcome]]),
      se      = sd(.data[[outcome]]) / sqrt(n),
      ci_low  = mean_y - 1.96 * se,
      ci_high = mean_y + 1.96 * se,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(
      segment     = factor(segment,
                           levels = c("No E-Mail", "Mens E-Mail", "Womens E-Mail")),
      recency_bin = factor(recency_bin,
                           levels = c("Warm", "Cooling", "Dormant", "Very dormant"))
    )

  control_means <- arm_means |>
    filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |>
    select(recency_bin, control_mean = mean_y)

  arm_means |>
    left_join(control_means, by = "recency_bin") |>
    mutate(
      abs_diff = mean_y - control_mean,
      pct_lift = abs_diff / control_mean * 100
    )
}
```

```{r analysis2-figure2-visit-faceted}
#| label: fig-2-visit-faceted
#| fig-cap: "Visit rate by arm, faceted by recency bin. Within each panel: 3 bars with 95% CIs, dashed reference line at the bin's No E-Mail visit rate, percentage-point and percent-of-baseline lift annotations on each email bar."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7

# Visit-rate version. Y-axis is visit rate (%, multiplied by 100 from 0/1).
# Lift annotations are in percentage points (NOT dollars), labeled "+X.X pp".
visit_means <- build_funnel_arm_means("visit") |>
  mutate(
    label = if_else(
      segment == "No E-Mail",
      "Control",
      sprintf("+%.1f pp\n(%+.0f%%)", abs_diff * 100, pct_lift)
    )
  )

control_visit_lines <- visit_means |>
  filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |>
  select(recency_bin, control_mean)

fig2_visit <- ggplot(visit_means,
                     aes(x = segment, y = mean_y * 100, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low * 100, ymax = ci_high * 100), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(data = control_visit_lines,
             aes(yintercept = control_mean * 100),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_high * 100, label = label),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3.2, lineheight = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ recency_bin, nrow = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "No E-Mail"     = "grey60",
    "Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
    "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25)),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Visit rate by recency bin and email arm",
    subtitle = "Share of customers who visited the site (95% CIs); dashed line = each bin's No E-Mail baseline",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Visit rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text     = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 10)
  )

ggsave("plots/figure2_visit_faceted_bars.png",
       fig2_visit, width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

```{r analysis2-figure2-conversion-faceted}
#| label: fig-2-conversion-faceted
#| fig-cap: "Conversion rate by arm, faceted by recency bin. Same idiom as the visit-rate figure but with conversion (purchase) rates. Bars are short because conversion is rare (~1% of customers); per-panel y-axis auto-scaling keeps the bars readable."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7

# Conversion-rate version. Same recipe as visit, different outcome column.
# Conversion is rarer than visit (~1% vs ~10%), so the absolute lifts are
# smaller in pp — but the percent-of-baseline lift annotation (+X%) keeps
# the relative magnitude readable.
conv_means <- build_funnel_arm_means("conversion") |>
  mutate(
    label = if_else(
      segment == "No E-Mail",
      "Control",
      sprintf("+%.2f pp\n(%+.0f%%)", abs_diff * 100, pct_lift)
    )
  )

control_conv_lines <- conv_means |>
  filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |>
  select(recency_bin, control_mean)

fig2_conv <- ggplot(conv_means,
                    aes(x = segment, y = mean_y * 100, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low * 100, ymax = ci_high * 100), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(data = control_conv_lines,
             aes(yintercept = control_mean * 100),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_high * 100, label = label),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3.2, lineheight = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ recency_bin, nrow = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "No E-Mail"     = "grey60",
    "Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
    "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25)),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Conversion rate by recency bin and email arm",
    subtitle = "Share of customers who purchased (95% CIs); dashed line = each bin's No E-Mail baseline",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Conversion rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text     = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 10)
  )

ggsave("plots/figure2_conversion_faceted_bars.png",
       fig2_conv, width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

```{r analysis2-figure2-spend-faceted}
#| label: fig-2-spend-faceted
#| fig-cap: "Spend per assigned customer by arm, faceted by recency bin. Visually identical to figure1_recency_spend_faceted_bars.png — saved under the figure2 prefix so the funnel-decomposition figure set (visit / conversion / spend) is complete."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7

# Spend version of the funnel. Visually identical to the Figure 1 alt
# (figure1_recency_spend_faceted_bars.png) because it's the same data,
# same plot. Re-saved under a figure2_* filename so the funnel set is
# complete and self-contained for presentation.
spend_means <- build_funnel_arm_means("spend") |>
  mutate(
    label = if_else(
      segment == "No E-Mail",
      "Control",
      sprintf("+$%.2f\n(%+.0f%%)", abs_diff, pct_lift)
    )
  )

control_spend_lines <- spend_means |>
  filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |>
  select(recency_bin, control_mean)

fig2_spend <- ggplot(spend_means,
                     aes(x = segment, y = mean_y, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(data = control_spend_lines,
             aes(yintercept = control_mean),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_high, label = label),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3.2, lineheight = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ recency_bin, nrow = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "No E-Mail"     = "grey60",
    "Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
    "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25)),
                     labels = dollar_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Spend per customer by recency bin and email arm",
    subtitle = "Mean spend per assigned customer (95% CIs); dashed line = each bin's No E-Mail baseline",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean spend per customer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text     = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 10)
  )

ggsave("plots/figure2_spend_faceted_bars.png",
       fig2_spend, width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

# Analysis 3: Campaign choice within each recency group

```{r analysis3-table6}
# Pivot Table 4 to one row per recency bin with separate columns for
# Mens, Womens, and the direct-difference contrast.
email_cost <- 0.01

ml <- table4 |>
  filter(comparison == "Mens E-Mail minus No E-Mail") |>
  select(recency_bin,
         mens_lift    = estimate,
         mens_ci_low  = ci_low,
         mens_ci_high = ci_high)

wl <- table4 |>
  filter(comparison == "Womens E-Mail minus No E-Mail") |>
  select(recency_bin,
         womens_lift    = estimate,
         womens_ci_low  = ci_low,
         womens_ci_high = ci_high)

dl <- table4 |>
  filter(comparison == "Mens E-Mail minus Womens E-Mail") |>
  select(recency_bin,
         mens_minus_womens         = estimate,
         mens_minus_womens_ci_low  = ci_low,
         mens_minus_womens_ci_high = ci_high)

# Apply the recommendation rules from the plan. Six cases handled in case_when
# below — order matters because each clause assumes the prior ones didn't fire.
table6 <- ml |>
  inner_join(wl, by = "recency_bin") |>
  inner_join(dl, by = "recency_bin") |>
  mutate(
    mens_profit    = mens_lift   - email_cost,
    womens_profit  = womens_lift - email_cost,
    best_action = case_when(
      mens_profit >= womens_profit ~ "Mens E-Mail",
      TRUE                         ~ "Womens E-Mail"
    ),
    best_ci_low = if_else(best_action == "Mens E-Mail",
                          mens_ci_low, womens_ci_low),
    # Apply the six rules in order. Both-positive direct-comparison cases first,
    # then one-positive cases, then both-non-positive.
    recommended_action = case_when(
      mens_profit <= 0 & womens_profit <= 0                                  ~ "No E-Mail",
      mens_profit > 0  & womens_profit > 0 & mens_minus_womens_ci_low > 0    ~ "Mens E-Mail",
      mens_profit > 0  & womens_profit > 0 & mens_minus_womens_ci_high < 0   ~ "Womens E-Mail",
      mens_profit > 0  & womens_profit > 0                                   ~ "Either email",
      TRUE                                                                   ~ best_action
    ),
    recommendation_strength = case_when(
      mens_profit <= 0 & womens_profit <= 0                                  ~ "Suppress",
      mens_profit > 0  & womens_profit > 0 & mens_minus_womens_ci_low > 0    ~ "Clear winner",
      mens_profit > 0  & womens_profit > 0 & mens_minus_womens_ci_high < 0   ~ "Clear winner",
      mens_profit > 0  & womens_profit > 0                                   ~ "Either email, no clear winner",
      best_ci_low > 0                                                        ~ "Strong send",
      TRUE                                                                   ~ "Tentative send, evidence uncertain"
    ),
    reason = case_when(
      mens_profit <= 0 & womens_profit <= 0 ~
        "Both arms have non-positive incremental profit at $0.01/send.",
      mens_profit > 0 & womens_profit > 0 & mens_minus_womens_ci_low > 0 ~
        "Both profitable; Mens significantly above Womens (direct CI excludes 0).",
      mens_profit > 0 & womens_profit > 0 & mens_minus_womens_ci_high < 0 ~
        "Both profitable; Womens significantly above Mens (direct CI excludes 0).",
      mens_profit > 0 & womens_profit > 0 ~
        "Both profitable; direct difference CI includes 0.",
      best_ci_low > 0 ~
        sprintf("%s is profitable; spend lift CI excludes 0.", best_action),
      TRUE ~
        sprintf("%s has positive expected profit but spend lift CI includes 0.",
                best_action)
    )
  ) |>
  select(recency_bin,
         mens_lift, mens_ci_low, mens_ci_high, mens_profit,
         womens_lift, womens_ci_low, womens_ci_high, womens_profit,
         mens_minus_womens, mens_minus_womens_ci_low, mens_minus_womens_ci_high,
         recommended_action, recommendation_strength, reason)

write_csv(table6, "tables/table6_policy_recommendations.csv")
```

```{r analysis3-figure3-policy-data}
# Shared data prep used by both Option A (verdict-titled bars) and
# Option B (incremental-profit bars). Long-format reshape from table6:
# one row per (recency bin, arm) with the arm's lift, CI, profit, and
# a 'Profitable / Not profitable' flag.
policy_long <- table6 |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(mens_lift, womens_lift),
    names_to  = "arm",
    values_to = "lift"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    arm = recode(arm,
                 mens_lift   = "Mens E-Mail",
                 womens_lift = "Womens E-Mail"),
    ci_low      = if_else(arm == "Mens E-Mail", mens_ci_low,  womens_ci_low),
    ci_high     = if_else(arm == "Mens E-Mail", mens_ci_high, womens_ci_high),
    profit      = if_else(arm == "Mens E-Mail", mens_profit,  womens_profit),
    profit_sign = factor(if_else(profit > 0, "Profitable", "Not profitable"),
                         levels = c("Profitable", "Not profitable"))
  )

# Verdict-first panel titles for Option A: lead with the action so the
# audience reads "WARM: Send Mens" before they read the bars.
verdict_titles <- table6 |>
  arrange(recency_bin) |>
  mutate(panel_title = case_when(
    recommended_action == "Mens E-Mail"   ~ sprintf("%s: Send Mens", recency_bin),
    recommended_action == "Womens E-Mail" ~ sprintf("%s: Send Womens", recency_bin),
    recommended_action == "Either email"  ~ sprintf("%s: Either email", recency_bin),
    recommended_action == "No E-Mail"     ~ sprintf("%s: Don't email", recency_bin),
    TRUE                                  ~ as.character(recency_bin)
  )) |>
  select(recency_bin, panel_title)

policy_long <- policy_long |>
  left_join(verdict_titles, by = "recency_bin") |>
  mutate(panel_title = factor(panel_title, levels = verdict_titles$panel_title))
```

```{r analysis3-figure3-optionA}
#| label: fig-3-optionA
#| fig-cap: "Figure 3 — Option A. Per-bin spend lift for Mens vs. Womens, faceted by recency bin with the verdict baked into the panel title. Red dashed line = $0.01 send cost; bars sit well above it everywhere."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7

# OPTION A — faceted bars, verdict in panel title.
fig3a <- ggplot(policy_long, aes(x = arm, y = lift, fill = arm)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = email_cost,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred") +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_high, label = sprintf("+$%.2f", lift)),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel_title, nrow = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
                               "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25)),
                     labels = dollar_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Policy recommendation by recency bin: lift evidence with verdict",
    subtitle = "Per-customer spend lift with 95% CIs; red dashed line = $0.01 send cost",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Spend lift ($/customer)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text     = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.text.x    = element_text(size = 10)
  )

ggsave("plots/figure3_policy_optionA_verdict_bars.png",
       fig3a, width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

```{r analysis3-figure3-optionB}
#| label: fig-3-optionB
#| fig-cap: "Figure 3 — Option B. Incremental profit per customer (spend lift minus $0.01 send cost) by recency bin and email arm. Green = profitable, red = unprofitable. All eight combinations are profitable in this data."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

# OPTION B — incremental profit bars, green/red profitability coding.
# group = arm is required so position_dodge splits Mens vs Womens within
# each recency_bin; without it, fill = profit_sign collapses both arms into
# a single group (since both are typically "Profitable") and the bars stack.
# Arm is labeled directly under each bar so the chart self-documents which
# bar is Mens vs Womens — no "left/right" key needed in the subtitle.
arm_short <- c("Mens E-Mail" = "Mens", "Womens E-Mail" = "Womens")

fig3b <- ggplot(policy_long,
                aes(x = recency_bin, y = profit,
                    fill = profit_sign, group = arm)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7,
           color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid",
             color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("$%.2f", profit)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = arm_short[arm], y = 0),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = 1.6, size = 3.0, color = "gray25") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Profitable"     = "#3FA34D",
                               "Not profitable" = "#D14B4B"),
                    drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(accuracy = 0.01),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.10, 0.20))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Incremental profit per customer (spend lift − $0.01 send cost)",
    subtitle = "Green = profitable, red = unprofitable. Two bars per recency bin: Mens E-Mail and Womens E-Mail.",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Incremental profit ($/customer)",
    fill     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top",
        axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12)))

ggsave("plots/figure3_policy_optionB_profit_bars.png",
       fig3b, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

# Analysis 4: Robustness and credibility checks

```{r analysis4-cell-sizes}
# Check 1: cell-size diagnostic. The visual answer lives in the
# Section 2 cell-counts figure (plots/figure_cell_counts.png),
# which already shows the n = 500 threshold line. Here we just compute
# the boolean flag for the results summary.
small_cells     <- table2 |> filter(n < 500)
any_small_cells <- nrow(small_cells) > 0
```

```{r analysis4-table7-winsor}
# Check 2: re-run Analysis 1 on spend_w99 (winsorized at the full-sample p99).
table7 <- data |>
  filter(!is.na(recency_bin)) |>
  group_by(recency_bin) |>
  group_modify(~ map_dfr(comparisons, function(cmp) {
    te_row(.x, "spend_w99", cmp$t, cmp$c, cmp$label)
  })) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    spend_variable = "spend_w99",
    winsor_cutoff  = spend_p99
  )

write_csv(table7, "tables/table7_winsorized_robustness.csv")
```

```{r analysis4-table8-balance}
# Check 3: covariate balance across the three arms. Numeric/binary covariates
# get per-arm mean and pairwise difference vs. No E-Mail; categorical
# covariates get per-level share and pairwise share difference vs. No E-Mail.
numeric_covs <- c("recency", "history", "mens", "womens", "newbie")
categorical_covs <- c("zip_code", "channel")

balance_numeric <- map_dfr(numeric_covs, function(v) {
  data |>
    group_by(segment) |>
    summarise(mean_val = mean(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
              n        = n(),
              sd_val   = sd(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups  = "drop") |>
    mutate(covariate = v, level = NA_character_)
})

ctrl_means <- balance_numeric |>
  filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |>
  select(covariate, ctrl_mean = mean_val, ctrl_sd = sd_val, ctrl_n = n)

balance_numeric <- balance_numeric |>
  left_join(ctrl_means, by = "covariate") |>
  mutate(
    diff_vs_control = mean_val - ctrl_mean,
    pooled_sd       = sqrt((sd_val^2 + ctrl_sd^2) / 2),
    smd             = diff_vs_control / pooled_sd,
    flag_imbalance  = if_else(segment != "No E-Mail" & abs(smd) > 0.10,
                              TRUE, FALSE)
  ) |>
  select(covariate, level, segment, n, mean_val, diff_vs_control, smd, flag_imbalance)

balance_categorical <- map_dfr(categorical_covs, function(v) {
  data |>
    count(segment, level = .data[[v]], name = "k") |>
    group_by(segment) |>
    mutate(share = k / sum(k), n = sum(k)) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(covariate = v)
})

ctrl_shares <- balance_categorical |>
  filter(segment == "No E-Mail") |>
  select(covariate, level, ctrl_share = share)

balance_categorical <- balance_categorical |>
  left_join(ctrl_shares, by = c("covariate", "level")) |>
  mutate(
    diff_vs_control = share - ctrl_share,
    smd             = NA_real_,
    flag_imbalance  = if_else(segment != "No E-Mail" & abs(diff_vs_control) > 0.02,
                              TRUE, FALSE)
  ) |>
  select(covariate, level, segment, n, mean_val = share, diff_vs_control, smd, flag_imbalance)

table8 <- bind_rows(balance_numeric, balance_categorical) |>
  arrange(covariate, level, segment)

write_csv(table8, "tables/table8_covariate_balance.csv")

imbalance_count <- sum(table8$flag_imbalance, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r analysis4-figure-balance}
#| label: fig-balance
#| fig-cap: "Per-arm mean (continuous/binary) or share (categorical) of each pre-treatment covariate, with 95% CIs. Overlapping CIs across the three arms is the signal that randomization worked."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7

# Per-arm balance figure in the style of root analysis1.qmd's robustness
# check 2. One panel per covariate (continuous/binary) or per category
# level (categorical). Three colored points + 95% CIs in each panel —
# the eye scans for non-overlapping intervals.
balance_plot_numeric <- map_dfr(numeric_covs, function(v) {
  data |>
    group_by(segment) |>
    summarise(
      n        = n(),
      estimate = mean(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE),
      se       = sd(.data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
      .groups  = "drop"
    ) |>
    mutate(covariate = v)
})

balance_plot_categorical <- map_dfr(categorical_covs, function(v) {
  data |>
    count(segment, level = .data[[v]], name = "k") |>
    group_by(segment) |>
    mutate(
      n        = sum(k),
      estimate = k / n,
      se       = sqrt(estimate * (1 - estimate) / n)
    ) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(covariate = paste0(v, ": ", level)) |>
    select(covariate, segment, n, estimate, se)
})

balance_plot_data <- bind_rows(balance_plot_numeric, balance_plot_categorical) |>
  mutate(
    ci_low    = estimate - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high   = estimate + 1.96 * se,
    segment   = factor(segment,
                       levels = c("No E-Mail", "Mens E-Mail", "Womens E-Mail")),
    covariate = fct_inorder(covariate)
  )

fig_balance <- ggplot(balance_plot_data,
                      aes(x = segment, y = estimate, color = segment)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ covariate, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "No E-Mail"     = "grey60",
    "Mens E-Mail"   = "steelblue",
    "Womens E-Mail" = "palevioletred"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Robustness check: covariate balance across arms",
    subtitle = "Per-arm mean (continuous/binary) or share (categorical) with 95% CIs",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Arm-level mean / share",
    color    = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

ggsave("plots/figure_robustness_balance.png",
       fig_balance, width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

# Results summary file

```{r results-summary}
# Build output/recency_results_summary.md with exact numbers from the tables.
# This is templating, not analysis — sprintf into preformatted text.

# Helpers for compact dollar/pp formatting in the summary.
fmt_dollar <- function(x) sprintf("$%.4f", x)
fmt_ci     <- function(lo, hi) sprintf("[%s, %s]", fmt_dollar(lo), fmt_dollar(hi))
fmt_pp     <- function(x) sprintf("%+.2f pp", x * 100)
fmt_pp_ci  <- function(lo, hi) sprintf("[%+.2f, %+.2f pp]", lo * 100, hi * 100)

# Pull the headline numbers we'll need for the summary.
overall <- table3
b1_max_mens <- table4 |>
  filter(comparison == "Mens E-Mail minus No E-Mail") |>
  slice_max(estimate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
b1_max_womens <- table4 |>
  filter(comparison == "Womens E-Mail minus No E-Mail") |>
  slice_max(estimate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

format_te <- function(row) {
  sprintf("%s in **%s**: %s (95%% CI %s, n_t=%d, n_c=%d)",
          row$comparison, row$recency_bin,
          fmt_dollar(row$estimate),
          fmt_ci(row$ci_low, row$ci_high),
          row$n_treatment, row$n_comparison)
}

policy_lines <- table6 |>
  mutate(line = sprintf("- **%s**: %s — %s. Mens lift %s %s; Womens lift %s %s.",
                        recency_bin,
                        recommended_action,
                        recommendation_strength,
                        fmt_dollar(mens_lift),
                        fmt_ci(mens_ci_low, mens_ci_high),
                        fmt_dollar(womens_lift),
                        fmt_ci(womens_ci_low, womens_ci_high))) |>
  pull(line)

# Compare raw vs winsorized lifts to see if winsorization changed direction
# or rough ranking. We do this by joining table4 to table7 on (bin, comparison).
winsor_compare <- table4 |>
  select(recency_bin, comparison, raw_estimate = estimate) |>
  inner_join(
    table7 |> select(recency_bin, comparison, w99_estimate = estimate),
    by = c("recency_bin", "comparison")
  ) |>
  mutate(direction_match = sign(raw_estimate) == sign(w99_estimate))

direction_agreement <- mean(winsor_compare$direction_match, na.rm = TRUE)

cell_size_ok <- !any(table2$n < 500)
imbalance_count <- sum(table8$flag_imbalance, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compose the summary markdown.
summary_md <- c(
  "# Recency Based Email Targeting Results",
  "",
  "## Data loaded",
  sprintf("- Rows: %d", n_rows),
  sprintf("- Columns: %d", n_cols),
  sprintf("- Duplicate rows: %d", n_dupes),
  sprintf("- Quality warnings: %s",
          if (length(quality_warnings) == 0) "none"
          else paste(quality_warnings, collapse = "; ")),
  "",
  "## Sample construction",
  sprintf("- Out-of-range recency (<1 or >12): %d", n_out),
  sprintf("- Customers excluded from bin analyses (unbinnable): %d", n_unbinned),
  sprintf("- Winsorization cutoff (p99 of full sample): $%.2f", spend_p99),
  "- Per-bin × arm cell counts available in tables/table2_recency_arm_counts.csv.",
  "",
  "## Baseline overall email effects",
  paste0("- ", overall$comparison[1], ": ",
         fmt_dollar(overall$estimate[1]), " ",
         fmt_ci(overall$ci_low[1], overall$ci_high[1])),
  paste0("- ", overall$comparison[2], ": ",
         fmt_dollar(overall$estimate[2]), " ",
         fmt_ci(overall$ci_low[2], overall$ci_high[2])),
  paste0("- ", overall$comparison[3], ": ",
         fmt_dollar(overall$estimate[3]), " ",
         fmt_ci(overall$ci_low[3], overall$ci_high[3])),
  "",
  "## Analysis 1: Spend lift by recency group",
  sprintf("- Largest Mens lift: **%s** at %s, %s.",
          b1_max_mens$recency_bin,
          fmt_dollar(b1_max_mens$estimate),
          fmt_ci(b1_max_mens$ci_low, b1_max_mens$ci_high)),
  sprintf("- Largest Womens lift: **%s** at %s, %s.",
          b1_max_womens$recency_bin,
          fmt_dollar(b1_max_womens$estimate),
          fmt_ci(b1_max_womens$ci_low, b1_max_womens$ci_high)),
  "- Full per-bin × comparison estimates: tables/table4_recency_spend_lift.csv.",
  "",
  "## Analysis 2: Causal funnel by recency group",
  "- ITT effects on visit, conversion, and spend per recency bin: tables/table5_recency_funnel_lift.csv.",
  "- Visit and conversion estimates are reported in percentage points (estimate_display column).",
  "- Spend estimates are dollars per assigned customer.",
  "",
  "## Analysis 3: Recommended targeting policy",
  policy_lines,
  "",
  "## Analysis 4: Robustness and credibility checks",
  sprintf("- All recency × arm cells have n ≥ 500: **%s**.", cell_size_ok),
  sprintf("- Winsorized vs raw direction agreement across (bin × comparison): **%.0f%%**.",
          direction_agreement * 100),
  sprintf("- Covariate-balance flags (numeric SMD > 0.10 or categorical share diff > 2pp): **%d**.",
          imbalance_count),
  "",
  "## Final managerial takeaway",
  sprintf("- Largest Mens E-Mail incremental spend: **%s** (%s).",
          b1_max_mens$recency_bin, fmt_dollar(b1_max_mens$estimate)),
  sprintf("- Largest Womens E-Mail incremental spend: **%s** (%s).",
          b1_max_womens$recency_bin, fmt_dollar(b1_max_womens$estimate)),
  "- Per-bin policy:",
  policy_lines,
  sprintf("- Robustness: cell sizes %s; winsorized direction agreement %.0f%%; %d balance flags.",
          if (cell_size_ok) "all sufficient" else "include cells below n=500",
          direction_agreement * 100,
          imbalance_count)
)

writeLines(summary_md, "output/recency_results_summary.md")
```
